questions.q: Which one of the following is not an example of a dendritic drainage system?
Describe the characteristics of a dendritic drainage system. (In about 50 words)
questions.answer: A dendritic drainage system is characterized by a tree-like pattern of streams and rivers. It has a single main stream and several tributaries. The main stream is the longest and the largest.

questions.q: What is the difference between a radial and a trellis drainage system? Explain with examples. (In about 75 words)
questions.answer: A radial drainage system is characterized by a single main stream with several streams radiating from it. An example is the Ganges-Brahmaputra system in India. A trellis drainage system is characterized by two or more main streams with several tributaries. An example is the Mississippi River system in the United States.

questions.q: A river in a hilly region has a steep gradient. What type of drainage system is likely to be formed in this region?
State any two characteristics of a trellis drainage system. (In about 25 words)
questions.answer: Two characteristics of a trellis drainage system are: (i) It has two or more main streams, (ii) Several tributaries.

questions.q: Distinguish between a dendritic and a trellis drainage system. Give two main differences. (In about 50 words)
questions.answer: Two main differences between a dendritic and a trellis drainage system are: (i) Number of main streams; (ii) Pattern of streams.

questions.q: What type of drainage system is formed when two or more rivers merge together?
Explain the concept of a dendritic drainage system. (In about 50 words)
questions.answer: A dendritic drainage system is a type of drainage system where a tree-like pattern of streams and rivers is formed. It has a single main stream and several tributaries.

questions.q: Describe the characteristics of a radial drainage system. (In about 25 words)
questions.answer: A radial drainage system has a single main stream with several streams radiating from it.

questions.q: Which type of drainage system is characterized by a single main stream with several tributaries?
